content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 867 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5660              322-5901              -322-6441              -322-6441

March 26, 1980

Walter E. Lancaster
Imperial County Assessor
939 Main Street
El Centro, CA 92243

Dear Mr. Lancaster:

This is in response to your letter of March 20, 1980 in which you ask if it would be a conflict of interest for you to activate your real estate brokers license.

Under the Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code Sections 81000, et seq., a public official's private financial interests do not per se create conflicts of interest. Instead, by virtue of holding certain financial interests, public officials are thereafter prohibited from taking certain actions as public officials. This prohibition is contained in Government Code Section 87100, which provides that:

No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest.

In further expansion of this concept, Government Code Section 87103 provides in part that:

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

Mr. Walter E. Lancaster                                    Page Two
March 26, 1980


     (b)  Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

     (c)  Any source of income, other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business on terms available to the public
without regard to official status, aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to, received by or promised to the public official
within twelve months prior to the time when the decision
is made; or

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.


     . . .

     Your county salary as Assessor is excluded from the term
"income" as used throughout the Political Reform Act and does not
therefore create any conflicts of interests.  See Government Code
Section 82030(b)(2).  If you engage in a private real estate
brokerage business, however, you would have to refrain from
participating in decisions which would foreseeably have a material
effect on your business in general or any sources of income
derived from your business.

     Note, however, that you are not required to disqualify
yourself from participating in decisions to the extent that your
participation is legally required for the decision to be made.
See Government Code Section 87101.  Determinations of when
participation is legally required, when a particular decision
will have a material effect on a financial interest, when such
effects are distinguishable from its effects on the public generally,
etc., can all be quite complicated.  Therefore, if you do decide
to reactivate your real estate brokers license, keep in mind
that we are available to provide you with advice concerning such
questions, either by phone or by letter.

     Finally, I can only comment on how the Political Reform Act
applies to your situation, but there may be other conflict of
interest laws applicable.  I know, for example, that some city
charters restrict the private business activities of city employees.

Mr. Walter E. Lancaster                                    Page Three
March 26, 1930


You may therefore want to consult your city attorney or a private
attorney for a review of the law outside the scope of the
Political Reform Act.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [illegible signature]
                                        Sarah T. Cameron
                                        Deputy Chief for
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Legal Division

STC:nrw

IMPERIAL COUNTY ASSESSOR '80

WALTER E. LANCASTER                                    W. GEORGE WILSON
ASSESSOR                                               CHIEF APPRAISER
(714) 352-3610 ext. 384                               (714) 352-3610 ext. 207

939 MAIN ST. EL CENTRO, CALIF. 92243

March 20, 1980




Fair Political Practices Commission
C/O The Conflict of Interest Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Sir:

     Would you be so kind as to give me your opinion in answer
to the following question?

     I am the elected Assessor of Imperial County, I would like
to activate my real estate brokers license, considering whether,
in your opinion, this would be a conflict of interest.

     Hoping to hear from you soon.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        WALTER E. LANCASTER
                                        Imperial County Assessor

WEL/jl
